skill.name: lark-doc
skill.description: 飞书云文档：创建和编辑飞书文档。从 Markdown 创建文档、获取文档内容、更新文档（追加/覆盖/替换/插入/删除）、上传和下载文档中的图片和文件、搜索云空间文档。当用户需要创建或编辑飞书文档、读取文档内容、在文档中插入图片、搜索云空间文档时使用；如果用户是想按名称或关键词先定位电子表格、报表等云空间对象，也优先使用本 skill 的 docs +search 做资源发现。
skill.summary: 飞书云文档：创建和编辑飞书文档。从 Markdown 创建文档、获取文档内容、更新文档（追加/覆盖/替换/插入/删除）、上传和下载文档中的图片和文件、搜索云空间文档。当用户需要创建或编辑飞书文档、读取...
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Lark/Feishu document management skill from the official larksuite CLI toolkit. The static scanner flagged many backtick shell executions and filesystem patterns, but these are FALSE POSITIVES - the skill uses lark-cli commands which are safe, hardcoded API calls to the legitimate Lark cloud platform. Network access is to official Lark API endpoints only.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Command Execution in Documentation Examples
medium_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in documentation files (e.g., references/lark-doc-create.md lines 4-649). These are FALSE POSITIVES - they are shell command examples showing how to invoke the lark-cli tool, which is the legitimate CLI for the Lark/Feishu cloud platform. The backticks are documentation syntax for showing command examples, not actual code execution in the skill itself.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Path Traversal in CLI Argument Examples
medium_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 'path traversal sequence' patterns in documentation files (e.g., references/lark-doc-create.md line 4). These are FALSE POSITIVES - the patterns detected (like '../') are part of documentation showing how to reference paths in the Lark system (e.g., '../lark-shared/SKILL.md'). These are not user input vectors or path manipulation vulnerabilities.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 'Hardcoded URL' patterns in documentation. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the URLs are legitimate Lark/Feishu API endpoints documented in the skill's reference files (e.g., open.feishu.cn). These are not secret endpoints or data exfiltration vectors.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: High File Entropy in Code Examples
medium_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 'high file entropy' patterns suggesting possible binary/encrypted content. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the entropy is from base64-encoded JSON examples in documentation, which is standard practice for showing API request/response formats. This is not obfuscation or malicious content.

low_findings.title: Low Risk: System Reconnaissance in Documentation
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 'system reconnaissance' patterns in documentation (e.g., 'hostname', 'whoami' mentions). These are FALSE POSITIVES - they appear in documentation comments explaining error messages or system behavior, not actual reconnaissance code.

content.value_statement: Streamline your Lark workspace by automating document creation, editing, and media management. This skill provides CLI-powered workflows for interacting with Lark/Feishu cloud documents through secure API integrations.
actual_capabilities: Create new Lark documents from Markdown content
Fetch and retrieve existing document content
Update documents with append, overwrite, replace, insert, or delete operations
Upload and download images and files within documents
Search cloud space for documents, wikis, and spreadsheets by name or keywords
Handle different document types including docx, doc, wiki, sheets, and whiteboards
limitations: Cannot directly edit existing whiteboard content - requires separate lark-whiteboard skill
Wiki links require additional lookup to resolve the actual document token before operations
Requires lark-cli binary and proper Lark/Feishu authentication setup
Document operations are limited to user's access permissions in Lark workspace

use_cases.title: Automated Documentation Generation
use_cases.description: Generate and update project documentation in Lark from Markdown templates, keeping team wikis synchronized with code changes.

use_cases.title: Content Migration and Bulk Updates
use_cases.description: Migrate content from other platforms to Lark or perform bulk updates across multiple documents using scripted workflows.

use_cases.title: Document Search and Discovery
use_cases.description: Search and locate documents, spreadsheets, and wiki pages across the Lark workspace using natural language queries.
use_cases.target_user: Knowledge workers managing large document repositories

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Lark document titled 'Project Status Report' with the following Markdown content: [insert your markdown here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Basic document creation from Markdown

prompt_templates.title: Fetch Document Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the content of this Lark document: [paste document URL] and summarize the key points

prompt_templates.title: Update Document with Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the document at [URL] by appending a new section with the attached image. The image file is located at [path]

prompt_templates.title: Search and Batch Process Documents
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for all documents containing 'Q4 report' in the title, then fetch each one and extract the budget figures into a summary

output_examples.input:
Create a new Lark document titled 'Meeting Notes' with markdown content '# Meeting Notes

## Attendees
- John
- Sarah'
output_examples.output: Document created successfully with token 'doxcnABC123XYZ'. URL: https://workspace.larksuite.com/docx/doxcnABC123XYZ

Document title: Meeting Notes
Content: # Meeting Notes

## Attendees
- John
- Sarah

## Action Items
- Review Q4 budget
- Schedule follow-up

output_examples.input: Search for documents with keyword 'project roadmap'
output_examples.output:
Found 3 results:
1. [DOC] 2024 Project Roadmap (doxcn123)
2. [WIKI] Product Roadmap Wiki (wikcn456)
3. [SHEET] Roadmap Timeline (shtcn789)

best_practices: Always verify document tokens by checking the URL format - wiki links require token resolution before use
Use the +search shortcut for discovering documents before performing operations on specific files
For whiteboard editing, record the board_token from creation response and use the lark-whiteboard skill
anti_patterns: Do not assume wiki URL tokens are directly usable - always resolve them via wiki.spaces.get_node first
Do not attempt to edit existing whiteboard content through docs+update - use the dedicated whiteboard skill
Avoid hardcoding document tokens in scripts - use search or pass tokens as variables

faq.question: What types of Lark documents can this skill handle?
faq.answer: This skill supports docx (new cloud docs), doc (legacy docs), wiki pages, sheets (spreadsheets), bitable (multi-dimensional tables), slides, and whiteboards. Each type has specific API endpoints and token handling requirements.

faq.question: How do I work with wiki links that have /wiki/ in the URL?
faq.answer: Wiki links cannot be used directly. First call lark-cli wiki spaces get_node with the wiki token to retrieve the actual obj_type and obj_token, then use the appropriate API for that document type.

faq.question: Can this skill edit whiteboard content?
faq.answer: No, this skill can create blank whiteboards within documents but cannot edit existing whiteboard content. Use the lark-whiteboard skill for whiteboard editing operations.

faq.question: What authentication is required?
faq.answer: The skill requires lark-cli to be installed and configured with proper Lark/Feishu credentials. Refer to lark-shared SKILL.md for authentication setup details.

faq.question: How do I search for spreadsheets specifically?
faq.answer: Use docs +search with keywords like 'spreadsheet', 'sheet', or 'report'. The search results include a type field indicating SHEET for spreadsheets, which you can then open with lark-sheets skill.

faq.question: What happens if I don't have permission to access a document?
faq.answer: The lark-cli will return an authorization error. Ensure you have proper workspace permissions and that the document is shared with your account or workspace.
